channel.title: KiddieDaily — Space
channel.description: Space exploration and astronomy news for families.

item.title: Nautilus Array to Track Missing Exoplanet Atmospheres
item.description: Exoplanet atmospheres have become prima targets for astrobiologists in the search for life beyond Earth. This is because exoplanet surfaces can’t be directly imaged yet, so astronomers must get creati

item.title: Astronomers Discover Another Galaxy With No Dark Matter
item.description: Astronomers using W. M. Keck Observatory on Maunakea, Hawaiʻi Island, have discovered the third known galaxy apparently lacking dark matter, part of a strange linear structure that may have formed dur

item.title: Mercury’s Thomas says received online abuse following WNBA suspension
item.description: Mercury forward Alyssa Thomas was suspended for a game after making contact with her fist on Caitlin Clark's throat.

item.title: Global firms and German startups compete in realizing nuclear fusion
item.description: Companies around the world are competing to see who can build the first commercially viable nuclear fusion reactor. German startups are also in the race, supported by major corporations and private in

item.title: Scientists say Earth Impacts May Have Delivered Microbes to the Clouds of Venus
item.description: New models suggest Earth microbes may briefly survive in Venus’s clouds.

item.title: Habitable Worlds Targets in New Star Activity Catalog
item.description: Searching for habitable worlds beyond our solar system consists of more than just having it orbit within its star’s habitable zone, which is the region where temperatures could be just right for liqui

item.title: Tennis superstar makes a big entrance at Wimbledon
item.description: The Grand Slam winner showed off her latest walk-on outfit for the Wimbledon crowds.

item.title: Chris Mason: Starmer's defence plan leaves crunching trade‑offs for Burnham to confront
item.description: The BBC's political editor Chris Mason reflects on the fallout from the Defence Investment Plan.

item.title: Galaxy Groups Hiding in the Universe’s Emptiest Places
item.description: Even the universe’s emptiest regions, the vast voids that make up most of the volume of space, are not entirely empty. A new study using the CAVITY survey hunts for galaxy groups hiding within these v

item.title: Space Lasers Show How Venezuela’s Earthquakes Reshaped the Earth’s Crust
item.description: New satellite imagery reveals how much terrain has shifted in the wake of the twin quakes.

item.title: A Star’s Death Throes Involves a Lot of Kicking
item.description: When stars like the Sun reach the end of their lives, the textbook story has them puffing up and quietly shedding their outer layers to leave a white dwarf behind. A new model suggests it is far less

item.title: Sun news: Bam! X flare! plus 5 M flares and a G1 geomagnetic storm
item.description: Sun news UPDATE for June 30, 2026: Bam!! X-flare!! Sunspot region AR4479 produced it. The post Sun news: Bam! X flare! plus 5 M flares and a G1 geomagnetic storm first appeared on EarthSky .

item.title: Venezuelan sky turns deep red as sun sets over Caracas
item.description: Venezuelan sky turns deep red as sun sets over Caracas

item.title: NASA audit puts Boeing's Starliner under an even bigger microscope: When will it fly astronauts again?
item.description: A new NASA audit says the agency will need to purchase more astronaut flights to the ISS by 2030 and focuses on issues with Boeing's Starliner spacecraft.

item.title: Epsilon Lyrae is the famous Double Double star
item.description: To the unaided eye, Epsilon Lyrae appears as one star. But it’s actually a system with at least five stars. Learn how to find it here. The post Epsilon Lyrae is the famous Double Double star first app

item.title: July birthstone: The rare and precious ruby
item.description: The July birthstone is the ruby, one of the most prized precious stones. It is also one of the hardest gemstones, and is chemically related to the sapphire. The post July birthstone: The rare and prec

item.title: Powerful X-class solar flare triggers radio blackouts across North America
item.description: A coronal mass ejection accompanied the X1.1 solar flare.

item.title: Largest and Most Detailed Picture Taken of Milky Way’s Center–With Millions of Stars and Thousands of Worlds
item.description: It took a new and special space telescope to parse out each one of these individual stars gleaming in a dizzying mass at the center of our own dear Milky Way galaxy. The European Space Agency’s

item.title: Earth farthest from sun – at aphelion – July 6, 2026
item.description: Earth farthest from sun - aka aphelion - on July 6. That's despite the fact that it's summer in the Northern Hemisphere. The post Earth farthest from sun – at aphelion – July 6, 2026 first appeared on

item.title: In a First For Science, A Satellite Has Identified What It's Seeing From Space
item.description: It's watching us. ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.
